title: Deaths in armed conflicts based on where they occurred, by type, 1989-2024
subtitle: Included are deaths of combatants and civilians due to fighting in [interstate](#dod:interstate-ucdp), [intrastate](#dod:intrastate-ucdp), [extrasystemic](#dod:extrasystemic-ucdp), [non-state](#dod:nonstate-ucdp) conflicts, and [one-sided violence](#dod:onesided-ucdp) that were ongoing over that time.
caption:
Deaths in armed conflicts based on where they occurred, by type, 1989-2024

Included are deaths of combatants and civilians due to fighting in interstate, intrastate, extrasystemic, non-state conflicts, and one-sided violence that were ongoing over that time.

This stacked horizontal bar chart displays the percentage distribution of conflict deaths by type across six geographic regions and the world total for the period 1989 to 2024. The legend identifies four categories: Intrastate (maroon), One-sided violence (gold), Non-state (orange), and Interstate (blue). For the World total, Intrastate conflicts account for the largest share at 51%, followed by One-sided violence at 30%, with Non-state and Interstate conflicts each comprising 9.7%. Regional variations are distinct. In Asia and Oceania, Intrastate conflicts dominate the chart at 87%, with One-sided violence at 8.9% and smaller shares for other types. The Middle East shows a similar pattern, with Intrastate conflicts making up 81% of deaths, while One-sided violence and Non-state conflicts account for 5.9% and 8.7% respectively. In Africa, One-sided violence represents the largest portion at 51%, followed by Intrastate conflicts at 37% and Non-state conflicts at 6.5%. The Americas display a different distribution, where Non-state conflicts constitute the majority at 71%, with Intrastate and One-sided violence at 17% and 11%. Europe is the only region where Interstate conflicts form the largest segment, accounting for 65% of deaths, compared to 29% for Intrastate and 5.6% for One-sided violence. The bars extend across a horizontal axis ranging from 0% to 100%, illustrating the proportional composition of conflict types within each specified location.
